name: Cranberry and Raisin Chutney
recipeIngredient: 2 Small Pears, Peeled, Cored, and Chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 2 cups water to a boil in a heavy large saucepan.
2. Add raisins. Remove from heat and let stand for 15 minutes. Drain, reserving half cup of the water.
3. Pour the reserved raisin water into the same saucepan. Add sugar and white wine vinegar and stir over medium-low heat until the sugar dissolves. Increase heat to medium and boil without stirring until the syrup turns golden brown, brushing down sides of the pan with a wet pastry brush.
4. Remove from heat. Mix in orange juice, orange peel, and fresh ginger. Add cranberries and cook until they pop, about 5 minutes. Stir in raisins, chopped pears, and toasted almonds and cook for 1 minute.
5. Cover and refrigerate the chutney until well chilled. The chutney can be made up to 2 days ahead of time; keep refrigerated.
Source: Bon Appetit Dec. 1991
